title: GovernanceFinding
description: A single portfolio governance finding, persisted as one element of the array at snapshots/governance.json on the repo-butler-data orphan branch. Produced deterministically by src/governance.js (standards-gap, policy-drift, tier-uplift) and src/dependabot-audit.js (dependabot-stale). Every finding carries a `remediation` plan (ADR-007) — an executor routing hint plus a change spec — consumed by the MCP get_governance_findings tool, the dashboard, the repo-butler-apply skill, and any future hosted agent.

type.description: The finding category, which determines the remaining fields.

priority.description: Remediation priority. standards-gap derives it from adoption rate; tier-uplift and dependabot-stale from severity; policy-drift is always medium.

tool.description: standards-gap only: the standard/tool key (e.g. code-scanning, dependabot-actions, contributing-guide).

repo.description: policy-drift, tier-uplift, dependabot-stale: the affected repository name.

nonCompliant.description: standards-gap only: repos missing the standard.

RemediationPlan.title: RemediationPlan
RemediationPlan.description: The portable remediation contract (ADR-007 Track B stage 1). Built deterministically by buildRemediationPlan() in src/governance.js — no LLM.

executor.description: Routing hint: `template` for findings the butler can write as a static file, `settings` for findings enabled via a repository-settings/ruleset write (ADR-009, e.g. code-review-bot), `agent` for findings needing tailored reasoning, `manual` for findings needing human judgement. A hint, not a guarantee the apply phase can act on it today.

targetFiles.description: Files the remediation would create or modify. Empty when the change is a repo-settings toggle or a human/legal decision rather than a file write.

intent.description: One-line statement of what the remediation does.

rationale.description: Why the finding fired, phrased deterministically from the finding's own fields.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Conditions that, once true, mean the finding is resolved.
